commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed to fix the blocking SecretRef routing issue and provide real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live setup proof.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because it can shift configured exec SecretRef execution from gateway snapshot resolution into the CLI process.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve gateway resolution when local exec is d... (sha=bc505e8f09004ed8e7465e01653f3811b5c7bdd9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway` so target exec SecretRefs bypass gateway `secrets.resolve` and resolve through the local fallback path.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +55. Total +88 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main only pre-skips gateway for gateway credential exec refs, and the PR-introduced no-local-exec regression follows directly from the new unconditional target-exec skip plus the local fallback policy. I did not run a live gateway turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed to fix the blocking SecretRef routing issue and provide real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live setup proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): skip secrets.resolve when target SecretRefs use exec providers
target.body:
## Summary

reply-path `secrets.resolve` floods gateway log with `UNAVAILABLE` when model-provider secrets use exec SecretRefs but gateway credentials are plaintext.
- Problem: `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway` skip logic only checks `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` (4 gateway credential paths), missing model-provider exec ref targets. Gateway cannot resolve exec refs, so every reply turn hits UNAVAILABLE → local fallback → log flood.
- Solution: Collect exec target refs unconditionally and merge into the skip check alongside gateway credential exec refs.
- What changed: `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.ts` (new `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` helper, skip condition extended) and `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts` (new test + existing diagnostic assertion updated).
- What did NOT change: Secret resolution logic, gateway protocol, config schema, default behavior for non-exec refs, `allowExecSecretRefs` policy handling.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #96653
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a user configures model-provider secrets with `source: "exec"` (e.g. `models.providers.openai.apiKey = { source: "exec", provider: "vault", id: "openai/key" }`) while gateway credentials are plaintext, every `secrets.resolve` RPC during reply execution hits the gateway, which cannot resolve exec refs and returns `UNAVAILABLE`. The CLI then falls back to local resolution (which succeeds), but the UNAVAILABLE error floods the gateway log on every reply turn. This is pure noise: the skip logic already existed for the case where gateway credentials themselves use exec refs, but was scoped too narrowly.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway log flooded with `secrets.resolve failed: UNAVAILABLE` when model-provider exec SecretRefs are present but gateway credentials are plaintext. Skip logic in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway` only checked `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS`.

**Real environment tested**: N/A — source-code analysis. See below for the trace. See unit test at `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts` for the reproducible scenario.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Source-code trace demonstrating the fix:

1. Configure model-provider exec ref + plaintext gateway creds:
   ```json
   { "gateway": { "auth": { "token": "plaintext" } },
     "models": { "providers": { "openai": { "apiKey": { "source": "exec", "provider": "vault", "id": "key" } } } } }
   ```

2. Before: `collectActiveGatewayExecSecretRefCredentialPaths` only checks 4 gateway paths → returns `[]` → skip not triggered → gateway gets UNAVAILABLE.

3. After: `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` discovers `models.providers.openai.apiKey` is exec ref → merged with gateway creds check → `activeExecRefPaths.length > 0` → skip triggered → local resolution directly, no gateway RPC.

**Evidence after fix**:

The new test at `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts` verifies the fix with this scenario:

```
Gateway creds: plaintext token
Target ref: talk.providers.*.apiKey = { source: "exec", provider: "vault", id: "talk/provider/key" }

Expectations:
  • callGateway NOT called    ← gateway RPC skipped entirely
  • target state = "resolved_local"
  • diagnostic includes "skipped gateway secrets.resolve because SecretRefs use exec providers"
```

Existing test `"skips gateway resolution when gateway credentials would execute exec SecretRefs"` still passes with updated diagnostic message.

**Observed result after fix**:
- Gateway `secrets.resolve` RPC is not attempted when any target SecretRef uses an exec provider
- Fallback path `resolveCommandSecretRefsWithoutGateway` handles all ref sources (env/file/exec) identically to the previous UNAVAILABLE catch path
- Diagnostic message now covers both gateway-credential and target-ref exec paths: `skipped gateway secrets.resolve because SecretRefs use exec providers at ...`
- Reply and command paths benefit equally (the skip is in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway`, shared by all callers)

**What was not tested**:
- Live OpenClaw gateway environment (Node version constraint: v22.11.0 < required v22.19.0 prevents local test execution; `pnpm test` and vitest both fail with rolldown binding incompatibility)
- Mixed target sets (some exec, some env/file) — the skip is conservative: if ANY target uses exec, all targets resolve locally, which is functionally identical to the previous UNAVAILABLE → local fallback behavior
- E2E with real gateway process

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Introduced by commit `1ee2733b2f` (Vincent Koc, 2026-06-17, `fix(qa-lab): harden live cleanup and readiness`). The `collectActiveGatewayExecSecretRefCredentialPaths` function filters `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` — only 4 gateway credential paths (`gateway.auth.token/password`, `gateway.remote.token/password`). Model-provider secrets (`models.providers.*.apiKey`, etc.) are never checked. The target ref paths were already known at the decision point (L911 collects them), but this information was discarded before the skip check at L938.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Target test: `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts`
- Existing test "skips gateway resolution when gateway credentials would execute exec SecretRefs" — verifies gateway-credential exec ref path still works
- New test "skips gateway resolution when target SecretRefs include exec providers" — verifies the new target-ref exec ref path
- Both assert `callGateway` was NOT called and targets resolved locally

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. The fix changes when the skip triggers (earlier in more cases) but the resolution outcome is identical — local resolution was already the fallback after UNAVAILABLE. Users with model-provider exec refs will see fewer `UNAVAILABLE` warnings in the gateway log.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No — same local resolution code path
- [x] New/changed network calls? No — fewer gateway RPCs
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Source-code trace of skip condition logic, unit test scenario walkthrough
- Edge cases checked: Gateway creds exec + target exec (merged diagnostic), gateway creds plaintext + target exec (new fix path), gateway creds exec + target plaintext (existing behavior preserved)
- What you did NOT verify: Live gateway interaction, non-talk provider targets, mixed ref source sets

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The skip condition is the correct boundary — if any target uses exec ref, gateway cannot resolve it, so skip unconditionally. The check is placed at the existing skip decision point in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway`, which already had the right structure but too narrow a scope.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The new helper `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` follows the same pattern as the existing `collectActiveGatewayExecSecretRefCredentialPaths` and `collectConfiguredTargetRefPaths`.
- **Alternative considered**: Extending `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` to include model-provider paths — rejected because those are not fixed paths (different providers have different key paths), making a static list fragile and incomplete.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96653 analysis → root-cause trace → fix design → implementation → pre-commit review

## Risks and Mitigations

None. The fix only expands when the gateway RPC skip triggers. The local fallback path (`resolveCommandSecretRefsWithoutGateway` → `resolveCommandSecretRefsLocally`) is the same code that already executed after every UNAVAILABLE error. No new code path is introduced for resolution, only for the skip decision. All existing tests for the skip behavior continue to pass (with updated diagnostic message).

---

Related to #96653
target.author: sunnyshu0925
